description:
You are an edge-computing engineer preparing a small IoT‐gateway project for hand-off to field technicians.  
    Inside the project workspace at 

        /home/user/projects/iot_gateway

    there is already a template file named  

        /home/user/projects/iot_gateway/config.template.env

    containing placeholder variables.  
    Your job is to turn this template into a real runtime environment and leave behind clear evidence that everything works.

    STEP-BY-STEP OBJECTIVES
    1. Make a copy of config.template.env named “.env” in the same directory and replace every placeholder with the final production values shown below (do NOT surround the values with quotes in the .env file):
           EDGE_DEVICE_ID=edge-raspi-001
           MQTT_BROKER_HOST=broker.iot.local
           MQTT_BROKER_PORT=1883
           SENSOR_POLL_INTERVAL=30
           FIRMWARE_VERSION=v2.5.1

    2. Create an executable Bash helper script called  

           /home/user/projects/iot_gateway/deploy.sh

       The script must:
           • source the .env file that sits next to it  
           • echo the five variables to STDOUT **in exactly the order shown below** and with precisely this key=value formatting (no extra spaces):

                EDGE_DEVICE_ID=<value>
                MQTT_BROKER_HOST=<value>
                MQTT_BROKER_PORT=<value>
                SENSOR_POLL_INTERVAL=<value>
                FIRMWARE_VERSION=<value>

           • The very first line of the script must be the she-bang “#!/usr/bin/env bash”.

    3. Run the script once and capture its STDOUT into a file called  

           /home/user/projects/iot_gateway/deployment.log

       The final deployment.log must therefore contain exactly five newline-terminated lines, in the same order produced by the script, with no banner, header or extra blank lines.

    4. Persist the firmware version for future login shells by appending a single line to the end of  

           /home/user/.bashrc

       The line must read:

           export FIRMWARE_VERSION="v2.5.1" #edge_deploy

       (The comment “#edge_deploy” must be present so automated grading can find the line easily.)

    5. Prevent the sensitive .env file from ever being committed to source-control:
           • If a file “/home/user/projects/iot_gateway/.gitignore” already exists, append a new line that contains only “.env”.
           • If it does not exist yet, create it with a single line “.env”.

    OUTPUTS THE GRADER WILL CHECK
    A. /home/user/projects/iot_gateway/.env  
       • Contains exactly 5 lines, each in KEY=value format shown above, terminated by UNIX newlines.  
       • Lines must appear in the same order listed in Objective 1.

    B. /home/user/projects/iot_gateway/deploy.sh  
       • Is executable (chmod +x).  
       • First line is the correct she-bang.  
       • Internal sourcing and echo order is correct.

    C. /home/user/projects/iot_gateway/deployment.log  
       • Exactly these 5 lines, in this order, newline-terminated:
             EDGE_DEVICE_ID=edge-raspi-001
             MQTT_BROKER_HOST=broker.iot.local
             MQTT_BROKER_PORT=1883
             SENSOR_POLL_INTERVAL=30
             FIRMWARE_VERSION=v2.5.1

    D. /home/user/.bashrc ends with the single export line described in Objective 4.

    E. /home/user/projects/iot_gateway/.gitignore contains a line that is literally “.env”.

    You do NOT need to initialise a git repository or perform a commit; simply ensure the files and their contents are correct.  All paths are absolute.  No sudo is required.  Good luck!
truth:
<!--
        Initial state provided to the agent:
        /home/user/projects/iot_gateway/           (directory exists)
        └── config.template.env                    (file exists with placeholders)
                 EDGE_DEVICE_ID=<DEV_ID>
                 MQTT_BROKER_HOST=<BROKER_HOST>
                 MQTT_BROKER_PORT=<BROKER_PORT>
                 SENSOR_POLL_INTERVAL=<POLL_INT>
                 FIRMWARE_VERSION=<FW_VER>

        No .env, deploy.sh, deployment.log or .gitignore yet.
        /home/user/.bashrc exists without any FIRMWARE_VERSION export line.

        Final state expected for grading:

        1. /home/user/projects/iot_gateway/.env
              EDGE_DEVICE_ID=edge-raspi-001\n
              MQTT_BROKER_HOST=broker.iot.local\n
              MQTT_BROKER_PORT=1883\n
              SENSOR_POLL_INTERVAL=30\n
              FIRMWARE_VERSION=v2.5.1\n

        2. /home/user/projects/iot_gateway/deploy.sh
              #!/usr/bin/env bash\n
              source "$(dirname "$0")/.env"\n
              echo "EDGE_DEVICE_ID=${EDGE_DEVICE_ID}"\n
              echo "MQTT_BROKER_HOST=${MQTT_BROKER_HOST}"\n
              echo "MQTT_BROKER_PORT=${MQTT_BROKER_PORT}"\n
              echo "SENSOR_POLL_INTERVAL=${SENSOR_POLL_INTERVAL}"\n
              echo "FIRMWARE_VERSION=${FIRMWARE_VERSION}"\n
        + executable bit set (0755 or 0700 acceptable).

        3. /home/user/projects/iot_gateway/deployment.log
              EDGE_DEVICE_ID=edge-raspi-001\n
              MQTT_BROKER_HOST=broker.iot.local\n
              MQTT_BROKER_PORT=1883\n
              SENSOR_POLL_INTERVAL=30\n
              FIRMWARE_VERSION=v2.5.1\n

        4. /home/user/.bashrc
              (original contents)…\n
              export FIRMWARE_VERSION="v2.5.1" #edge_deploy\n

        5. /home/user/projects/iot_gateway/.gitignore
              .env\n
              (possibly other pre-existing lines)

        The grader will assert:
          • Correct key/value pairs in .env, correct order.
          • deploy.sh exists, has correct she-bang, is executable, contains sourcing and echo lines.
          • deployment.log matches exactly the five expected lines.
          • .bashrc ends with the export line containing #edge_deploy.
          • .gitignore contains a line exactly “.env”.
    -->
name: task_000000_dc5cf133
